page: Microsoft Azure D2lds_v7 is a low-memory, local disk-enabled virtual machine belonging to the Dldsv7 server family. Powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor operating at 4.2 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server features 2 vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with 2 threads. The hardware configuration includes 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It is equipped with 118 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, though it does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or GPU accelerators.

Performance benchmarks reveal a stark contrast between raw single-threaded execution and memory subsystem throughput. The server excels in single-threaded compression tasks, achieving top-tier results with 35.35 GB/sec in gzip compression and 14.66 GB/sec in bzip3 decompression. However, the memory architecture exhibits limitations, with uncached large-block read bandwidth at 24.65 GB/sec and write bandwidth at a poor 12.55 GB/sec. This bottleneck impacts memory-intensive operations, as evidenced by weak performance in Redis SET operations at 4.21 million operations per second and static web serving throughput at 4.9 GB/sec. Small-model LLM inference text generation registers at an average 90.16 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D2lds_v7 offers a budget-friendly entry point for compute-focused workloads that do not require large memory footprints. The inclusion of 118 GB of local storage provides a resource-dense option for temporary data handling without incurring additional storage attachment costs. Due to the low memory-to-vCPU ratio and constrained memory bandwidth, this server is poorly suited for large databases or memory-heavy applications. It is best utilized for lightweight single-threaded processing, basic web serving, utility scripting, and small-scale computational tasks that leverage its high-frequency Intel processor and advanced vector extensions.
description: Microsoft Azure D2lds_v7 is a low-memory, compute-oriented virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs from a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor and 4.0 GB of RAM. Lacking a GPU, it includes 118 GB of local storage and supports premium storage. Benchmarks show top-tier single-threaded performance, reaching 35.35 GB/sec in gzip compression and 14.66 GB/sec in bzip3 decompression. However, memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, with uncached write speeds limited to 12.55 GB/sec and weak Redis throughput. This configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-focused, low-memory workloads but unsuitable for memory-intensive databases. It is best fit for lightweight single-threaded processing, basic web serving, and utility scripting.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D2lds_v7 is a low-memory virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs on a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 118 GB of local storage, optimized for lightweight compute tasks.

tagline: A low-memory, high-frequency compute instance featuring dedicated Intel processing and local storage for lightweight single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated 2 vCPU Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz
4.0 GB of system memory and 118 GB of local storage
Top-tier single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression performance
Constrained memory write bandwidth of 12.55 GB/sec
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks and basic web serving
